user_message: Can you work with me on territory planning for Tailspin Toys? The CSA flagged this as at-risk yesterday.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-27 17:06 EST
Subject: Re: Re: territory planning Timeline

Just checking in on the territory planning proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-20 12:30 EST
Subject: territory planning - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with territory planning. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-01 13:48 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Tailspin Toys territory planning

Our team encountered some challenges with territory planning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Tailspin Toys: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-K trap (Consolidated view when separate-thread view would be more useful); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-K failure mode (Consolidated view when separate-thread view would be more useful)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-K -- Consolidated view when separate-thread view would be more useful
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
